channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Donde hasta las flores provocan miedo
item.description: Los generales de Birmania han brutalizado al país durante décadas. Pero han llegado a temer las guirnaldas como signo de resistencia.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech and Daniel Berehulak
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat

media:content.media:description: The bombs come at any time. The only constant is that they will.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. Military Says It Has Blocked Iranian Ports
item.description: After ship trackers had shown that Iran-linked vessels were transiting the strait, the military claimed that all commercial traffic to Iranian ports had been cut off.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Running a 5K in a Van
item.description: Influencers go to great lengths — even in small spaces — to get views? How far would you go?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: How a Times Reporter Has Covered a Turbulent Period in Latin America
item.description: Frances Robles, an international correspondent for The New York Times, has reported on the region for more than 25 years.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat

media:content.media:description: The reporter Frances Robles, right, with the Rev. Thomas Ngigi at St. Theresa’s parish, a waterfront church in Black River, Jamaica, that was badly damaged by Hurricane Melissa last year.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 15, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Super Typhoon Sinlaku Slams Saipan, Causing Widespread Damage
item.description: But there were no early reports of casualties from the storm, which was named Sinlaku and was moving northwest of the region.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

media:content.media:description: Debris scattered on Wednesday in Saipan, Northern Mariana Islands, as the super typhoon Sinlaku moved through.

item.title: Late Night Laughs at Explanations of Trump’s Jesus ‘Joke’
item.description: Ronny Chieng poked fun at JD Vance’s defense of President Trump posting an image of himself as a Christ-like figure.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Palabra del día: ‘effervescence’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 27 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: effervescence
item.description: This word has appeared in 27 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: En Corea del Sur hay una crisis en la medicina de urgencias
item.description: Cada año, cientos de pacientes tienen dificultades para acceder a la atención de urgencias, a pesar de que el país cuenta con un sistema de salud de primera clase. Para algunos, los retrasos han sido fatales.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young

media:content.media:credit: Jun Michael Park para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Los paramédicos deben llamar primero al hospital y obtener permiso para acudir. Pueden ser rechazados por varias salas de urgencias.

item.title: How China’s Weapons Transfers to Iran Have Evolved Over Decades
item.description: China has denied claims this month by U.S. intelligence that it might have shipped arms to Iran. In recent years, China has supported Iran with dual-use parts.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Military delegates at an annual legislative session in Beijing last month.

item.title: The World Has Forgotten Sudan, but I Haven’t
item.description: The spirit of the Sudanese people cannot be crushed.
item.dc:creator: Mohammed Ahmed

item.title: Pope Leo to Trump: ‘I Have No Fear’
item.description: A harsh attack by the president drew a quick response from the pontiff, a reminder of his uniquely moral voice.
item.dc:creator: Daniel J. Wakin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: La caída de Orbán es una oportunidad de oro
item.description: Los votantes demostraron que la democracia puede ser restaurada pacíficamente por el pueblo.
item.dc:creator: Stefano Bottoni

item.title: Pope Leo’s Challenge in Cameroon: Show African Catholics How Much They Matter
item.description: Catholicism is growing fast on the continent, yet Africans play a comparatively small role in church leadership. Cameroon, which Leo XIV will visit Wednesday, shows the divide.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich

media:content.media:description: Posing after Mass on Sunday at St. Joseph Metropolitan Cathedral in Bamenda, Cameroon. For some Catholics in the country, a visit by Pope Leo XIV on Wednesday will be a reminder of disparities in the church.

item.title: War Exposes Gaps in India’s Clean Energy Push
item.description: The country’s creaking electricity grid is lagging behind its push for renewable sources — just as its growing economy hungers for oil stranded by war.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli and Suhasini Raj

media:content.media:credit: Saumya Khandelwal for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A floating solar farm in Visakhapatnam, India.

item.title: Billy Crystal to Return to Broadway With Solo Show ’860’ About Losing His Home in L.A. Fires
item.description: The actor-comedian said he will return to Broadway this fall with a new solo show called “860,” named for the address of his destroyed family home.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson

media:content.media:description: Billy Crystal at this year’s Academy Awards ceremony in March.

item.title: Quote of the Day: A Police Chief’s $4.5 Million Gambling Secret: ‘It’s on the App’
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, April 15, 2026.

item.title: Prosecutors Make Surprise Visit to Fed as Pirro Defends Investigation
item.description: The Justice Department’s criminal inquiry into the Federal Reserve threatens to delay the confirmation of the next chair.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith and Glenn Thrush

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An overview showing construction at the Federal Reserve last year. The $2.5 billion renovation project is at the center of an investigation into the central bank.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers: April 15, 2026
item.description: Adam Wagner’s Wednesday puzzle coalesces into a fun solve.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Aronow

media:content.media:credit: Sylvia Jarrus for The New York Times

item.title: Corrections: April 15, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, April 15, 2026.

item.title: Israel’s ‘One-State Reality’
item.description: Hope for a two-state solution between Israel and Palestine is preventing the world from reckoning with the “one-state reality” of the present, the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein argues.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Alex Cooper and Alix Earle Are Fighting. Or Are They?
item.description: Two popular influencers have exchanged jabs on social media.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta

media:content.media:credit: From left, Neilson Barnard/Getty Images; Daniel Cole/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Alix Earle, left, and Alex Cooper.

item.title: JD Vance Heckled In Antiwar Protest at Turning Point USA Event
item.description: Vice President JD Vance appeared to express sympathy with critics of the war with Iran: “I recognize that young voters do not love the policy we have in the Middle East, OK. I understand.”
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Mr. Vance, right, addressed the audience outbursts Tuesday, agreeing that “Jesus Christ does not support genocide” and defending the Trump administration’s Middle East policies

item.title: Vance Says Pope Leo Should Be More Careful When Talking About Theology
item.description: The vice president, who is Catholic, took issue with Pope Leo XIV’s statement that disciples of Christ are “never on the side of those who once wielded the sword and today drop bombs.”
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance spoke at a Turning Point USA event in Athens, Ga., on Tuesday.

item.title: Ship ‘Spoofing’ in Strait of Hormuz May Compound Confusion
item.description: A new pattern of deceptive activity by some vessels around the critical waterway suggests the new American blockade is changing how some ships linked to Iran are behaving.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A cargo ship off the coast of Ras al-Khaimah, United Arab Emirates, on Monday.

item.title: Rubio Hosts Israel and Lebanon for Rare Meeting Shadowed by U.S.-Iran War
item.description: The gathering ended with encouraging words, even as Israel continued to refuse to halt its military campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meeting with officials from Lebanon and Israel at the State Department in Washington on Tuesday.

item.title: House Passes Air Safety Bill, Setting Up Clash with Senate
item.description: Senate leaders say key provisions fall short of what is necessary to prevent aircraft from midair collisions.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian

media:content.media:description: The U.S. Capitol on Tuesday.

item.title: Muere Mario Ríos Montt, obispo defensor de derechos humanos en Guatemala y hermano de un dictador genocida
item.description: Lideró la oficina de derechos humanos de la Iglesia católica en Guatemala incluso cuando su hermano, el ex dictador militar del país, era investigado por genocidio.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:description: El obispo Mario Ríos Montt en 2000 en una conferencia de prensa sobre las atrocidades contra los derechos humanos cometidas por los militares en Guatemala. Afirmó que lo que a él le interesaba era la verdad, pasara lo que pasara, y que la verdad era la verdad.

item.title: Swalwell’s Exit Injects ‘Chaos’ Into California Governor’s Race
item.description: Democratic candidates see sudden voter interest in the sleepy contest as a campaign opportunity. All are scrambling for support from former backers of Eric Swalwell.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Theodore Schleifer

media:content.media:description: Eric Swalwell’s departure seemed most likely to benefit the two Democrats who had been polling closest to him.

item.title: Captains of Boat That Capsized Near San Diego Plead Guilty to 4 Deaths
item.description: The boat was carrying 19 migrants from Mexico to Southern California when it overturned in May 2025, the authorities said. Two of the victims were children.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:description: The capsized boat last year at Torrey Pines State Beach in San Diego.

item.title: Taylor Frankie Paul Will Not Face New Domestic Violence Charges
item.description: Prosecutors in Utah investigated after the reality star’s ex-boyfriend told the police she had scratched, shoved and struck him during a fight in February.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.category: The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives (TV Program)

item.title: Trump’s Blockade Risks Upending an Emerging Détente With China
item.description: In a thinly veiled critique of the war in Iran, China’s leader said the world could not risk reverting “to the law of the jungle.”
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: President Trump meeting with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, in South Korea last October. They will meet again next month.

item.title: Hochul Proposes Tax on N.Y.C. Second Homes That Are Worth $5 Million
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul, who has opposed raising some taxes, favored a “pied-à-terre” luxury tax because it largely targets the ultrawealthy who primarily live outside New York City.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes

media:content.media:credit: Liam Kennedy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ms. Hochul repeatedly expressed her unwavering opposition to Mr. Mamdani’s tax proposals.

item.title: Like Anthropic, OpenAI Will Share Latest Technology Only With Trusted Companies
item.description: The maker of ChatGPT announced the limited release of GPT-5.4-Cyber, a technology designed to find security holes in software.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz

media:content.media:credit: Aaron Wojack for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: OpenAI says its technology is adept at finding bugs and other vulnerabilities in software.

item.title: Trump Officials Pledge Swift Completion of Controversial Gas Pipeline
item.description: The $1 billion project will run for about 23 miles under Raritan Bay in New Jersey and New York Harbor. Environmental groups oppose it.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Howard

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Trump administration officials gathered on Tuesday in Brooklyn for the groundbreaking for a natural gas pipeline, including Chris Wright, left, the energy secretary; Lee Zeldin, third from left, the Environmental Protection Agency administrator; and Doug Burgum, second from right, the interior secretary.

item.title: Pentagon Says It Attacked Another Boat in the Pacific, Killing 4
item.description: It was the second strike in two days, in a campaign against those the United States accuses of drug smuggling.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Southern Command, led by Gen. Francis L. Donovan of the Marine Corps, announced the latest strike on Tuesday.

item.title: US Blockade Stops Iran-Linked Ships From Crossing Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. Central Command said that six vessels had complied with directions to turn around and re-enter an Iranian port.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Vessels anchored in Muscat, Oman, last month. U.S. Central Command said a blockade on Iranian-linked ships would be enforced from the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea, both east of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Gallego Dismissed Rumors of a ‘Flirty’ Swalwell, Highlighting a Culture of Silence
item.description: The admission by Senator Ruben Gallego that he had heard, but disbelieved, rumors about Eric Swalwell and women showed the attitude on Capitol Hill toward men accused of behaving badly.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

media:content.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Ruben Gallego at the Capitol on Tuesday. He spent considerable time with Mr. Swalwell and chaired his short-lived 2020 presidential campaign.

item.title: L.G.B.T.Q. Community Expresses Hesitancy About Hungary’s New Leader
item.description: After years of anti-L.G.B.T.Q. policies, members of the queer community in Hungary were cautiously hopeful that Peter Magyar’s win over Viktor Orban’s Fidesz party could bring about change.
item.dc:creator: Monika Cvorak, Jamie Leventhal and Daniel Fetherston

item.title: Surgeon Who Removed Wrong Organ From Patient Is Charged in His Death
item.description: Dr. Thomas Shaknovsky tried to persuade his colleagues in the operating room that the liver he removed from a 70-year-old patient was a spleen, according to Florida’s Health Department.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai

media:content.media:description: William and Beverly Bryan.

item.title: Lebanon and Israel Hold Rare In-Person Talks
item.description: While the talks between Israel and Lebanon in Washington did not yield a cease-fire agreement, both sides agreed to “launch direct negotiations” after having “productive discussions,” according to a statement from the U.S. State Department.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meeting with officials from Lebanon and Israel at the State Department in Washington on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump’s Blasphemy Is a Warning
item.description: Presidents can fight with popes. God is another matter.
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by The New York Times; source images by SOPA Images and Ron Sanford, via Getty Images

item.title: Justice Dept. Moves to Vacate Jan. 6 Convictions for Far-Right Extremists
item.description: Defending the convictions would likely have required administration officials to assert that far-right groups were acting on behalf of President Trump on Jan. 6, 2021.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer

media:content.media:description: President Trump released Stewart Rhodes, the founder and leader of the Oath Keepers, from prison but did not grant him a full pardon.

item.title: Carney Suspends Gas Tax in Canada as War Drives Up Fuel Costs
item.description: The prime minister said that his government was also investing in ways to boost oil and gas production.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada at a news conference in Ottawa, on Tuesday.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Talk, but Fighting Continues
item.description: Also, a Ukrainian city is bouncing back with Denmark’s help. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: The site of an Israeli airstrike that hit a residential building in Beirut last week.

item.title: The Museums That Helped Power Atlanta’s Rise Are Still Pushing Ahead
item.description: For 100 years, the Atlanta History Center and the High Museum of Art have expanded and diversified, not unlike the metropolis itself.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas

media:content.media:credit: Kendrick Brinson for The New York Times

item.title: Trump Says 2nd Round of U.S.-Iran Talks Could Take Place This Week
item.description: The president did not say who would represent the United States in a potential next round of direct talks, though he ruled out being personally involved.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Monday.

item.title: Air New Zealand to Introduce ‘Skynest’ Sleep Pods on Some Long-Haul Flights
item.description: Air New Zealand will begin offering economy passengers four-hour naps in private pods on certain long-haul flights. Will other carriers follow its lead?
item.dc:creator: Gabe Castro-Root

media:content.media:description: Air New Zealand demonstrated its Skynest pods at an event in New York on Tuesday. The airline will debut the service in November on New York-Auckland flights.

item.title: A New Exhibition at New York’s Natural History Museum Honors Fossil Hunters
item.description: In a new and ongoing exhibition, the American Museum of Natural History highlights the findings of Mark Norell and other fossil hunters responsible for its most important discoveries.
item.dc:creator: Mark A. Stein

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Paleontologists and their team excavate an Ankylosaur, an armored dinosaur, at Camel’s Humps in the Gobi Desert in Mongolia in 2005.
